hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are used as feature extractors within a graph neural network framework to encode gas-specific plasma behavior as node attributes, then the resulting model will more accurately predict mixture etching rates under out-of-distribution conditions (unseen process parameters and gas compositions) compared to a standard feedforward neural network trained directly on mixture data, because the graph structure explicitly captures pairwise gas interactions while the pre-trained encoders provide physically meaningful representations that generalize beyond the training distribution.
